feed.title: Daily Quote
feed.subtitle: One quote a day, with a Wikiquote link to the author when one exists

entry.title: “Prepare for the worst, expect the best and take what comes. This is fatalism.” — Anonymous
entry.content: Prepare for the worst, expect the best and take what comes. This is fatalism. — Anonymous

entry.title: “Who do you think's better looking people in the north or people in the south?” — Umar ibn Al-Khattāb (R.A)
entry.content: Who do you think's better looking people in the north or people in the south? — Umar ibn Al-Khattāb (R.A)

entry.title: “All the good are friends of one another.” — Zeno of Citium
entry.content: All the good are friends of one another. — Zeno of Citium

entry.title: “Be sure you put your feet in the right place, then stand firm.” — Abraham Lincoln
entry.content: Be sure you put your feet in the right place, then stand firm. — Abraham Lincoln

entry.title: “There are three signs of a knowledgeable person: knowledge, forbearance and silence.” — Ali ibn Abi Talib (R.A)
entry.content: There are three signs of a knowledgeable person: knowledge, forbearance and silence. — Ali ibn Abi Talib (R.A)

entry.title: “Human beings are makers, usually of a mountain out of a molehill.” — Mokokoma Mokhonoana
entry.content: Human beings are makers, usually of a mountain out of a molehill. — Mokokoma Mokhonoana

entry.title: “The injuries we do and those we suffer are seldom weighed in the same scales.” — Aesop
entry.content: The injuries we do and those we suffer are seldom weighed in the same scales. — Aesop

entry.title: “The element of the unexpected and the unforeseeable is what gives some of its relish to life and saves us from falling into the mechanical thralldom of the logicians.” — Winston Churchill
entry.content: The element of the unexpected and the unforeseeable is what gives some of its relish to life and saves us from falling into the mechanical thralldom of the logicians. — Winston Churchill

entry.title: “If you wish to know the mind of a man, listen to his words.” — Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
entry.content: If you wish to know the mind of a man, listen to his words. — Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

entry.title: “Sadness may be part of life but there is no need to let it dominate your entire life.” — Byron Pulsifer
entry.content: Sadness may be part of life but there is no need to let it dominate your entire life. — Byron Pulsifer

entry.title: “The absent are never without fault, nor the present without excuse.” — Benjamin Franklin
entry.content: The absent are never without fault, nor the present without excuse. — Benjamin Franklin

entry.title: “Our greatest glory is not in never failing but rising everytime we fall.” — Confucius
entry.content: Our greatest glory is not in never failing but rising everytime we fall. — Confucius

entry.title: “Fame always goes with the principles.” — Baltasar Gracián
entry.content: Fame always goes with the principles. — Baltasar Gracián

entry.title: “Throughout the centuries there were men who took first steps, down new roads, armed with nothing but their own vision.” — Ayn Rand
entry.content: Throughout the centuries there were men who took first steps, down new roads, armed with nothing but their own vision. — Ayn Rand

entry.title: “Tolerance starts when you practise it.” — Proverb
entry.content: Tolerance starts when you practise it. — Proverb

entry.title: “The man who spends his time choosing one resort after another in a hunt for peace and quiet, will in every place he visits find something to prevent him from relaxing. The story is told that someone complained to Socrates that travelling abroad had never done him any good and received the reply: "What else can you expect, seeing that you always take yourself along with you when you go abroad?‟” — Seneca
entry.content: The man who spends his time choosing one resort after another in a hunt for peace and quiet, will in every place he visits find something to prevent him from relaxing. The story is told that someone complained to Socrates that travelling abroad had never done him any good and received the reply: "What else can you expect, seeing that you always take yourself along with you when you go abroad?‟ — Seneca

entry.title: “I want to sing like the birds sing, not worrying about who hears or what they think.” — Rumi
entry.content: I want to sing like the birds sing, not worrying about who hears or what they think. — Rumi

entry.title: “War is not an adventure. It is a disease. It is like typhus.” — Antoine de Saint-Exupéry
entry.content: War is not an adventure. It is a disease. It is like typhus. — Antoine de Saint-Exupéry

entry.title: “In all of us, even in good men, there is a lawless wild-beast nature, which peers out in sleep” — Sokrates
entry.content: In all of us, even in good men, there is a lawless wild-beast nature, which peers out in sleep — Sokrates

entry.title: “Every person is a fool in somebody's opinion.” — Danish Proverb
entry.content: Every person is a fool in somebody's opinion. — Danish Proverb

entry.title: “Religions, which condemn the pleasures of sense, drive men to seek the pleasures of power. Throughout history power has been the vice of the ascetic.” — Bertrand Russell
entry.content: Religions, which condemn the pleasures of sense, drive men to seek the pleasures of power. Throughout history power has been the vice of the ascetic. — Bertrand Russell
